big_picture: India hikes ATF prices by 115% for non-scheduled carriers and charters amid the West Asia crisis, with the government considering relief measures. The move is part of the country's response to the ongoing crisis in the region. The government cut special additional excise duty on petrol by Rs 10 per litre to shield consumers from rising crude prices. The Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026, sparked a sharp political divide, with the BJP defending the move as necessary for national security and the opposition flagging minority rights concerns. CPI(M) MP John Brittas slammed the government for treating FCRA data as 'National Secrecy' and claimed that all his queries were disallowed in the Rajya Sabha. The Supreme Court quashed a dowry harassment case against a woman's parents-in-law and sister, emphasizing that unexplained delay in initiating criminal proceedings can be fatal in matrimonial disputes. Tennis player Leander Paes joined the BJP ahead of the West Bengal polls.

Legal.summary:
The Delhi High Court issued a notice on a plea by the Enforcement Directorate against the acquittal of former Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal in cases over skipping summons related to the liquor policy. 

The Supreme Court quashed a dowry harassment case against in-laws, emphasizing that unexplained delay in initiating criminal proceedings can be fatal in matrimonial disputes. 

Justice N Nagaresh spoke at his farewell address, stating that the Kerala High Court has formidable women lawyers and that it was the same court where the first woman Supreme Court Judge was appointed.

Politics.summary:
The Rajya Sabha has cleared the Finance Bill 2026, with the Finance Minister stating that the excise duty cut is intended to not burden citizens, a move the opposition considers a poll tactic. 

The Lok Sabha has passed amendments to the insolvency law, with the Finance Minister saying it will help save viable businesses. 

The Rajya Sabha has held a discussion on the CAPF (General Administration) Bill, with the opposition previously stating that the Bill goes against constitutional provisions.

India's financial year starts from 1st April due to its alignment with agricultural cycles and administrative needs, a structure inherited from the British. 

Toolsvilla is a platform in India that simplifies the buying of agricultural equipment for farmers and small business owners. 

The Finance Ministry states that measures are in place to ensure a seamless flow of rural credit, with the Reserve Bank of India working to boost liquidity for the economy.

Shares of Garden Reach Shipbuilders Engineers jumped 20% after the company reported a record turnover of Rs 6,400 crore, up 26% year-on-year. 

India increased ATF prices by 115% for non-scheduled carriers and charters, driven by the ongoing West Asia war, with the government considering relief measures.

Gold prices have increased to their highest in nearly two weeks due to a weaker dollar after US President Donald Trump said something, with MCX gold rate jumping above ₹1.52 lakh per 10 gm and silver prices jumping by ₹1,100 per kg. 

Indian stock markets have jumped over 2% on hopes of de-escalation in West Asia, with global markets also rallying. 

The US war on Iran was driven by overconfidence from a swift victory in Venezuela, with the Trump administration's miscalculation showing that lessons were not learned from Vietnam, Afghanistan, and Ukraine.

A report by the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) has highlighted issues such as overcrowding and inadequate bathing facilities for women in Odisha prisons.

Finance.summary:
The Government of India has announced the sale of Government Securities worth ₹29,000 crore through an underwriting auction to be held on April 02, 2026. 

The Money Market Operations as of March 30, 2026, reported a weighted average rate range in the overnight segment. 

A plan by Jharkhand Chief Minister Hemant Soren to build a ₹69-crore residence, called 'Sheesh Mahal', has triggered a political row in the state.

Infrastructure.summary: The price of Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) has more than doubled to a record Rs 2.07 lakh per kilolitre, which may lead to a rise in airfares. The jet fuel price for domestic flights has been hiked partially, while the price for international flights has more than doubled.
Infrastructure.top_article_title: To cushion Indian flyers, jet fuel price for domestic flights hiked only partially; price for international flights more than doubles

Energy.summary: Commercial LPG prices in Delhi have increased by Rs 195.5 per 19-kg cylinder, with the new price being Rs 2,078.5. This price hike is attributed to the ongoing Iran war.
Energy.top_article_title: Commercial LPG becomes costlier: Prices hiked by Rs 195.5 per 19-kg cylinder

Labour.summary:
Workers across India are going on strike today, protesting against the four new labour codes that they believe will worsen their economic situation. The protest comes at a time when workers are already bearing the brunt of the economic fallout from the US-Israel war on Iran.

Telangana has passed a landmark Bill that allows the docking of salary of employees who neglect their parents.

Oracle has sent layoff emails to 12,000 employees in India, informing them that today is their last day of work, with the company having nearly 1.62 lakh full-time employees in May 2025.
Labour.top_article_title: Panipat’s battle for labour rights shows why workers across India are going on strike today

Health.summary:
In the Rajya Sabha, three BJP MPs raised concerns about medicine pricing, women's care economy, and tribal development. 

The Census 2027 will be conducted entirely in digital mode, starting from today, after being delayed due to COVID-19. 

The BJP's manifesto for the Kerala Assembly polls 2026 includes promises of AIIMS and a high-speed rail network as part of their 'Vikasita Keralam Vision'.
Health.top_article_title: MPs raise issues of medicine pricing, women’s care economy, tribal development in Rajya Sabha

Civil Liberties.summary:
The Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026, has sparked a political divide in India, with the BJP defending it as necessary for national security and opposition parties raising concerns about minority rights. 

India recorded the highest number of internet shutdowns among democracies in 2025, according to a report by Access Now, which documented 65 such incidents. 

A Malayalam filmmaker, Ranjith, has been remanded to judicial custody in a sexual harassment case, with the director claiming he has been "framed" in the case.
Civil Liberties.top_article_title: FCRA amendment sparks row; Oppn flags minority rights concerns, BJP cites national security

Technology.summary:
Bharat Innovates, an initiative led by the Ministry of Education, is planning to take 100-120 Indian deeptech startups to France from June 14, with the help of Future Wealth's $50M VC fund. 

Bharti Airtel's data centre arm, Nxtra Data, is set to raise $1 billion, increasing its valuation to $3.1 billion due to its data centre push.

Leader Nasiruddin Patwary of the NCP in Bangladesh believes that Sheikh Hasina's government has given too much space to India, and the current BNP government is following a similar path.
Technology.top_article_title: Bharat Innovates is taking Indian startups global; Future Wealth’s $50M VC fund

Trade.summary:
India's Centre has granted a one-time customs duty and AIDC relief for SEZ-made goods sold in the domestic market from April 2026 to March 2027, with certain conditions and caps. 

The Registrar General and Census Commissioner of India has warned Chief Secretaries of States that lapses in duty by Census officials can result in up to three years in prison, as per Section 11 of the Census Act. 

Gold and silver prices in India have increased due to gains in international bullion prices, with current prices available for cities including Delhi and Mumbai.
Trade.top_article_title: India grants one-time customs duty relief for SEZ goods sold in domestic market

Environment.summary:
India's CO2 emissions grew at the slowest pace in over two decades in 2025, with a 0.5% increase in the second half of the year, according to analysis by Carbon Brief. 

Justice B V Nagarathna emphasizes the importance of corporate accountability and environmental justice, stating that profit should not be allowed to stand alone.
Environment.top_article_title: India’s CO2 emissions grew at the slowest pace in over two decades in 2025: Analysis

Urban.summary:
India has launched its largest census to count over 1.4 billion people, collecting data on housing, economics, and caste that could reshape welfare policies. 

The Indian residential real estate market is experiencing a shift in demand, with buyers seeking larger, gated homes and premium amenities, leading to rising prices. 

Chief Secretary Sanjay Gupta has denied any involvement in a Himachal Pradesh housing project, stating that two retired bureaucrats are defaming him.
Urban.top_article_title: India begins biggest census to count 1.4 billion people, caste data to reshape politics

DeepTech.summary:
Anthropic's AI coding tool, Claude Code, accidentally exposed its full source code due to a packaging error, but no user data or core AI systems were affected. 

DeepSeek, a Chinese AI startup, experienced its longest outage, lasting over seven hours, but the reason for the outage remains undisclosed.
DeepTech.top_article_title: Anthropic's AI coding tool, Claude Code, accidentally reveals its source code; here's what happened

Welfare.summary:
Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta dismissed claims about the 'Pink Saheli Card', stating that some political people are spreading rumors because the AAP is annoyed by welfare schemes. 

Madhya Pradesh saw a return to dung-cake stoves, reversing the progress made by the Ujjwala scheme which provided smoke-free kitchens. 

Interest rates on small savings schemes, including the Sukanya Samriddhi Scheme, remain unchanged.
Welfare.top_article_title: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta dismisses rumours about 'Pink Saheli card', says AAP just 'annoyed' by welfare schemes

Education.summary: Jammu University is criticized for removing "Muslim" thinkers from its syllabus, which is seen as turning the classroom into an echo chamber and erasing crucial figures in Indian and South Asian history.
Education.top_article_title: What Jammu University gets wrong about removing ‘Muslim’ thinkers from the syllabus

Telecom.summary:
India's average monthly mobile data consumption has surpassed 31 GB, with 5G traffic increasing by 70% according to a report. Additionally, India now has the world's second-largest 5G subscriber base, 5G data consumption, and 5G FWA user base.

The Department of Telecommunications is likely to extend the deadline for SIM binding for messaging platforms like WhatsApp and Telegram to December to help resolve technical issues.
Telecom.top_article_title: India's average monthly mobile data consumption crossed 31 GB mark, 5G traffic grew by 70%: Report

articles.title: GRSE shares soar 20% to hit upper circuit. Here's why
articles.summary: Shares of Garden Reach Shipbuilders   Engineers jumped over 16% after the company reported a record FY26 turnover of Rs 6,400 crore, up 26% year-on-year. articles.title: Co-Owner Cannot Be Denied Enhanced Compensation Granted To Other Co-Owners Under Same Acquisition: Himachal Pradesh High Court
articles.summary: The Himachal Pradesh High Court has held that a co-owner whose land has been acquired is entitled to the same compensation as another co-owner, even if the former did not independently seek a reference under the Land Acquisition Act. The Court observed that once fair compensation is judicially determined for land acquired under the same notification, denying the same to similarly placed co-owners on technical grounds would amount to discrimination. A division bench comprising Justice Gurmeet Sin

articles.title: 'Digital Vigilantism': Delhi High Court Pulls Up Media, Actress Richa Chadha For Defamatory Posts Against Man Over Indigo Flight Incident
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court recently pulled up various media platforms including a journalist and actress Richa Chadha for their social media posts labelling a man a “molester” over allegations of sexual harassment arising out of an in-flight incident. Justice Vikas Mahajan said that the narratives set by the media houses and digital platforms clearly breached the contours of the FIR, and that the publications did not merely report the allegations in the FIR but they prematurely adjudicate the matter.
articles.raw_summary: The Delhi High Court recently pulled up various media platforms including a journalist and actress Richa Chadha for their social media posts labelling a man a “molester” over allegations of sexual harassment arising out of an in-flight incident. Justice Vikas Mahajan said that the narratives set by the media houses and digital platforms clearly breached the contours of the FIR, and that the publications did not merely report the allegations in the FIR but they prematurely adjudicate the matter.

articles.title: Earning Mother Need Not Be Impleaded In Child Maintenance Claim Against Father But Court Must Assess Joint Income: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court has observed that an earning mother need not be formally impleaded as a party in a maintenance plea filed by a child against her/his father. A bench of Justice Madan Pal Singh , however, directed that in such cases, the trial court must consider the financial capacity of both earning parents while determining the final maintenance amount based on the principle of shared parental responsibility. The single judge passed this order while hearing a criminal revision petition

articles.title: How Toolsvilla Is Simplifying Agricultural Equipment Buying for Farmers
articles.summary: Did you know there’s a dedicated platform in India that’s changing how people buy equipment? For a long time, farmers and small business owners didn’t really have many options. Buying machinery usually meant going to the nearest dealer and choosing from whatever was available. There wasn’t much information to compare, and most decisions were made quickly often without knowing if it was the right fit.

articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court has observed that under Mohammedan Law, a divorce takes effect from the date a husband pronounces talaq and a subsequent court decree confirming it is merely declaratory in nature. articles.title: "Geopolitics won’t derail markets forever": Devina Mehra urges investors to stay the course
articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions in West Asia are creating market uncertainty, with crude oil prices remaining high. India's oil import dependence remains a key concern, though opportunities may emerge during this volatile period.

articles.title: Gold prices rise above Rs 1.51 lakh/10 grams, silver drops around 1% amid hopes of Iran-US war de-escalation

articles.title: India levies export duty on diesel, ATF to secure domestic supply
articles.summary: India has levied ₹21.5/l duty on diesel and ₹29.5/l on ATF exports as global fuel prices soar. The step seeks to discourage export-driven gains and safeguard domestic supply.

articles.title: Trade Relief Measures for Exporters
articles.summary: Due to ongoing geopolitical uncertainties and logistical disruptions, Reserve Bank of India has been receiving representations from various stakeholders regarding challenges in adhering to the timelines for realisation of export proceeds. In this regard, Reserve Bank, vide Press Release No. 2025-2026/1510 dated November 14, 2025 , had advised extension in the time period for realisation and repatriation of full export value of goods/software/services exported from India from nine months to fifte

articles.title: Supervisory Data Quality Index for Scheduled Commercial Banks  (December 2025)
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released Supervisory Data Quality Index (sDQI) for SCBs for December 2025 . It measures data quality in terms of the Accuracy, Timeliness, Completeness and Consistency in the submission of returns. The objective of sDQI is to assess the adherence to the principles enunciated in the Master Direction on Filing of Supervisory Returns 2024 . (Brij Raj)             Chief General Manager Press Release: 2025-2026/2361

articles.title: RBI defers implementation of the Amendment Directions on Capital Market Exposures to July 1, 2026
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India had issued the final Amendment Directions on Capital Market Exposure on February 13, 2026 , after due consideration of the feedback received as part of public consultation. These Amendment Directions were aimed primarily to (i) provide an enabling framework for banks to finance acquisitions by Indian corporates; (ii) rationalise the limits for lending by banks to individuals against shares, units of REITs, InvITs, etc. and (iii) put in place a more principle-based frame

articles.title: Liquor Consumption Is Personal Choice, But Shops Causing Nuisance Must Be Checked: Madras High Court
articles.summary: Setting aside a recreational club's alcohol license, the Madras High Court observed that while consuming alcohol is the personal choice of a person, no nuisance should be caused to the persons living in a locality by granting license to operate liquor shop. “ It is to be noted that the consumption of liquor is an individual choice of a person. However, such liquor shops causing nuisance or posing threat to the residents of that locality must be looked into,” the court said. The bench of Justice

articles.title: The Inimitable C.K. Daphtary
articles.summary: C.K. Daphtary is a jewel of the legal profession in India. He occupied the high offices of Solicitor General and Attorney General and by his superior powers of advocacy and the warmth of his personality carved for himself a unique niche amongst the immortals in the world of law. It is appropriate to remember him and salute his memory on his birth anniversary. Chander Kishan Daphtary affectionately called C.K or Chandubhai was born on 1st April, 1893. It was Hanuman Jayanti according to the Hindu

articles.title: Electricity Supply Can't Be Denied To Occupant Due To Ownership Dispute: Andhra Pradesh High Court
articles.summary: While emphasising that electricity is a basic necessity, the Andhra Pradesh High Court has observed that mere pendency of civil litigation does not constitute grounds for discontinuation of power supply to a consumer, unless there exists an order explicitly restraining supply of electricity. Justice Ninala Jayasurya was dealing with a writ petition filed by a 62-year old woman who had purchased a property in 2000, and was running a hotel (commercial purpose) on the premises while residing there

articles.title: Wife's Professional Competence, Earning Potential Can't Be Ignored While Deciding Maintenance: Allahabad High Court
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court has observed that maintenance can't be granted, ignoring the professional competence and earning potential of the wife-claimant. A bench of Justice Madan Pal Singh thus reduced the monthly maintenance awarded to a wife under Section 125 CrPC, taking into account the wife's professional qualifications and past employment as a radiologist The single judge modified a Family Court order, lowering the maintenance amount from Rs 18,000 to Rs 12,000 per month. articles.title: CHRIST (Deemed to be University), Pune Hosts THEMIS 6th Moot Court Competition 2026
articles.summary: The School of Law, CHRIST (Deemed to be University), Pune Lavasa Campus , successfully hosted its flagship event, THEMIS – 6th Moot Court Competition, 2026, from March 27 to 29, 2026. The competition featured a prize pool of ₹3,00,000 , along with individual awards for Best Speaker, Best Researcher, and Best Memorial. The 2026 moot proposition focused on contemporary issues in constitutional law, federalism, and data governance, encouraging participants to engage with evolving legal challenges.

The 2026 moot proposition focused on contemporary issues in constitutional law, federalism, and data governance, encouraging participants to engage with evolving legal challenges.

articles.title: Call For papers; Delhi Journal For Law And Justice Invites Submissions For 2026 Issue
articles.summary: The Delhi Journal for Law and Justice (DJLJ) is the student-run journal of Law Centre-II, Faculty of Law, University of Delhi . It is a double-blind peer-reviewed academic journal focused on publishing original, high-quality research in the field of law and justice. The journal encourages discussion on important legal issues and promotes critical engagement with legal theories and practices. It contributes to the ongoing discussion on justice, governance, rights, and duties in India and beyond.

articles.title: NLIU Bhopal Team Wins 5th National Moot Court Competition, 2026
articles.summary: The National Law Institute University (NLIU), Bhopal has announced that its team comprising Anaya Muchhal and Kriti Sharma as speakers, along with Vidhushi Jaiswal as researcher, emerged as the winner of the 5th National Moot Court Competition, 2026, organised by Army Law College, Pune. The competition was held on March 27–28, 2026 in Pune and focused on themes of Constitutional Law and the Information Technology Act, while also addressing contemporary legal issues relating to the Digital Person

articles.title: Writ Petition Not Maintainable Before Final Order In Court Martial Process: Delhi High Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has held that a writ petition challenging disciplinary and court martial proceedings is not maintainable before the passing of a final order. A Division Bench of Justices V. Kameswar Rao and Manmeet Pritam Singh Arora observed, “...it is a settled position of law that till such time a final order is passed in the proceedings of this nature, any interference without waiting for the final decision on the proceedings by the High Court in a writ petition is uncalled for.” Relian

articles.title: Driving Licence Not Fake Merely Because It Was Not Converted To Smart Card: Delhi High Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has held that a driving licence cannot be treated as fake merely because it was in a booklet form and had not been converted into a smart card. Justice Neena Bansal Krishna thus dismissed an appeal filed by an insurance company against a decree in favour of the insured. The bench upheld the trial court's decision directing The New India Assurance Company Ltd. to pay ₹13,77,500 along with interest at 12% per annum to M/s Kapoor Diesels Garage Pvt. articles.title: Artificial Intelligence In Courts: Promise, Peril, And Path Ahead
articles.summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools are increasingly relied upon and being used in Indian courts by lawyers. articles.title: Telangana relied on RBI borrowings for most of FY25: CAG report
articles.summary: Telangana's finances in 2024-25 saw extensive use of RBI's Special Drawing Facility and Ways and Means Advances. The state government borrowed Rs 27,730 crore via SDF and Rs 64,188 crore through WMA. Overdrafts totaling Rs 37,457 crore were also availed. This reliance on short-term borrowing mechanisms highlights financial management challenges. The CAG report detailed these transactions.

Its operating revenue fell 11% in fiscal 2025, though net loss narrowed.

articles.title: Courier export cap removed to boost ecomm shipments, cut logistics delays
articles.summary: Starting April 1, India has abolished the ₹10 lakh threshold on courier exports, a shift aimed at propelling e-commerce forward and supporting small enterprises. The introduction of a quick return mechanism for unclaimed parcels is also noteworthy, designed to alleviate shipping delays and enhance overall delivery efficiency for cross-border commerce.

articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's net short position in forwards rose to $77 billion in February. This move impacts the central bank's capacity to protect the rupee. Despite these challenges, India's economy shows strong momentum.

articles.title: Iran war shock: Middle East conflict could cut 1 ppt from India’s FY27 GDP outlook
articles.summary: Iran War: A persistent Middle East conflict could reduce India's real GDP growth by 1 percentage point and increase retail inflation by 1.5 percentage points from baseline estimates. This impact stems from disruptions to global oil and energy markets, affecting employment-intensive sectors and aggregate demand. The government may need to implement countercyclical policies and augment the Economic Stabilization Fund.

articles.title: India diesel exports jump 20% in March amid Iran war

India also increased domestic LPG production to compensate for lost imports. Overall refined product exports saw a slight decline.

articles.title: Pulses import duties likely to stay unchanged
articles.summary: New Delhi is set to maintain current import duties on pulses. The government will also extend the duty-free period for tur and urad. This move aims to prevent price increases. articles.title: India's net international liability comes down by $10.9 billion in the third quarter
articles.summary: India's financial obligations to the world decreased by $10.9 billion in the third fiscal quarter. This improvement was driven by Indian residents increasing their overseas assets more than foreigners increased their assets in India. The ratio of India's international assets to liabilities saw a positive shift. Indian residents invested more abroad in direct investments and deposits.

The trend indicates a continued expansion in industrial undertakings.

articles.summary: Rising crude oil prices due to West Asian tensions could significantly push up India's inflation by 55-60 basis points per $10 barrel increase in FY27. articles.title: Government to likely halt disbursement under ECMS if design, Six Sigma quality not met: Ashwini Vaishnaw
articles.summary: The government is set to review its electronics manufacturing scheme. Beneficiaries must invest in product design and achieve Six Sigma standards. Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw warned that non-compliance will lead to halted disbursements and approvals. The industry has 15 days to present plans for design, talent, and local sourcing. This move aims to boost high-quality, self-reliant electronics manufacturing in India.

A shift towards logistics and supply chain activities is also evident.

articles.title: Australia seeks 'another crack' at trade deal with India in coming weeks
articles.summary: India and Australia are set to resume trade negotiations in the coming weeks, with Canberra eager to advance a bilateral agreement. While both nations are optimistic, negotiations are anticipated to be complex and unfold over multiple stages.

articles.summary: India's economic outlook is bolstered by robust domestic demand and improving high-frequency indicators, including strong auto sales and GST collections. External vulnerabilities remain a concern due to reliance on Middle Eastern energy and its importance for Indian exports and remittances.

articles.title: What changes will RBI’s new rules bring to digital payments from April 1?
articles.summary: India's digital payments are getting a security upgrade. From April 1, the Reserve Bank of India will require two-factor authentication for all digital transactions. This strengthens India's payment system and aligns it with global security standards.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | West Asia subsea cable threats: What could happen to India’s internet amid Iran war?
articles.summary: Subsea cables carrying India's data face damage risk in a volatile region. While a total blackout is unlikely, rerouting traffic could lead to noticeable delays for users and businesses across the country.
articles.raw_summary: Subsea cables carrying India's data face damage risk in a volatile region. While a total blackout is unlikely, rerouting traffic could lead to noticeable delays for users and businesses across the country.

articles.title: What is the minimum amount to start trading in the Indian stock market?
articles.summary: You can start investing in the Indian stock market with as little as ₹100, but experts suggest ₹5,000–₹10,000 for diversification and cost efficiency. Platforms like HDFC Sky are making investing easier for beginners.
